options: Rule by one national government only
Dividing power between the national government and the states
questions.explanation: Power is shared between the federal and state governments.
National matters are handled by the federal government.

questions.q: Which is usually a state or local responsibility?
What does the Supremacy Clause do when valid federal and state laws conflict?
questions.explanation: The Constitution makes federal law the highest law of the land.

questions.q: What are powers shared by both federal and state governments called?
questions.explanation: Taxing is an example of a concurrent power.
